name: ETH 4h Retest Hold Timeout v1
edge_mechanism: a structural break is retested and held, but exposure is cut by time if expansion does not appear quickly, avoiding slow bleed continuation trades
thesis: The cycle still wants retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough. ETH 4h is a better control lane for this concept than TAO because its chop forces the timeout logic to do real work rather than relying on raw trend carry. The edge is the held retest plus strict time discipline, not passive hope.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h retest holds remain too sparse or if time-based de-risking still cannot prevent bleed, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks a local boundary, retests that level, holds it, and should expand quickly if the continuation is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Real continuation tends to reveal itself quickly after an accepted retest. If expansion does not appear fast, the trade is often stale or wrong; timeout de-risking should cut that failure mode.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit for this management test because its flatter regime forces the timeout logic to prove it can reduce bleed rather than just riding a TAO trend.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: ETH may simply be too flat, turning the retest-hold grammar into repeated false positives even with timeout discipline.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but timeout is too aggressive, extend the de-risk clock slightly before loosening the retest filter.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A fair number of retest attempts, many early scratches, and only a minority of fast-expanding continuations that justify the structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h clears density and timeout de-risking materially reduces slow-bleed continuation losses.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or a profile where retest entries still bleed despite the timeout rule.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because retest-hold grammar is simpler than sparse delayed-retest theater

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit control lane for testing whether timeout logic can rescue retest continuation in a flatter regime.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough retest-hold events and timeout de-risking reduces bleed.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h remains too flat or too sparse for the structure to matter.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with quick feedback on whether continuation is real.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same timeout-protected retest grammar survives on the transition-heavy primary lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h preserves retest-hold continuation with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h confirms the concept is too noisy even with timeout control.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher amplitude and slightly higher count than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same timeout management behaves differently in a trend-capable lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows coherent retest-hold continuation with fewer slow bleeds.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the idea just piggybacks trend and teaches little about the timeout edge.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.85 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.85 ATR expansion
